channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: DMK and Congress Finalise Seat-Sharing Deal: Congress to Contest 28 Assembly Seats in Tamil Nadu Elections
item.description: The ruling Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) in Tamil Nadu, led by Chief Minister M.K. Stalin, has successfully concluded a seat-sharing agreement with its long-time ally, the Indian National Congress, ...

item.title: Mark Carney’s Approach Raises Concerns About Canada’s Commitment to Gender Equality
item.description: As International Women’s Day approaches, concerns are growing over Canada’s commitment to gender equality under Prime Minister Mark Carney. Despite the 30th anniversary of the United Nations Beijing D...

item.title: Montreal Student Pilot Faces Charges After Home Crash Injures Two
item.description: A student pilot has been formally charged following an incident on Montreal’s South Shore where his aircraft crashed into a residential home. The crash resulted in injuries to both the pilot and a man...

item.title: Global Human Rights Judges Raise Alarm Over Growing Use of Force and Weakening Rule of Law
item.description: During the Fourth International Human Rights Forum held in Arusha on March 4, 2026, judges from Africa, Europe, and the Americas expressed serious concern over the increasing use of force worldwide. T...

item.title: Aaron Spencer Wins Republican Primary for Lonoke County Sheriff Amid Pending Murder Trial
item.description: Aaron Spencer, who is facing second-degree murder charges, won the Republican primary for Lonoke County sheriff in Arkansas, receiving 53.5% of the vote. Longtime incumbent John Staley received 26.5%,...

item.title: Hotel in Karachi issued e-challan for long-stolen vehicle due to system glitch
item.description: In Karachi, a five-star hotel received an unexpected e-challan of Rs 10,000 for a car that was stolen nearly 28 years ago. The automated Traffic Regulation and Citation System (TRACS), launched on Oct...

item.title: Three Youths Drown in Mombasa Boat Capsize During Ocean Festival Amid Safety Concerns
item.description: Three young men from Mombasa's informal settlements tragically drowned in the Indian Ocean on Friday during the East African Ocean Festival at Tudor area. The victims, Stephen Ngoa (23), Caleb Otieno ...

item.title: HMS Dragon Deployment to Cyprus Delayed Amid Rising Middle East Tensions
item.description: The British Type 45 destroyer HMS Dragon, recently out of maintenance in Portsmouth, is preparing to deploy to Cyprus but is not expected to sail until next week. The vessel has been reassigned to pro...

item.title: Chinese Nationals Recruit Filipinos in Defense Sector for Espionage Activities
item.description: Since 2023, Philippine authorities have been investigating cases where Chinese nationals recruited young Filipinos working in or connected to government defense and security agencies, such as the Depa...

item.title: Apple launches $899 MacBook Neo with iPhone chip for budget-conscious users
item.description: Apple has launched the MacBook Neo, its most affordable laptop ever, priced at $899. This marks a significant push by the company into the low-end laptop market, targeting budget-conscious consumers a...

item.title: Enugu Government Declares Mechanic Village Land Off-Limits to Unauthorized Buyers
item.description: The Enugu State Government don issue serious warning to anybody wey dey try market or buy land wey dem earmarked for the Enugu Mechanic Village at Akegbe Ugwu. The warning come from the Commissioner f...

item.title: Nigeria Opens West Africa’s First Dedicated Sickle Cell Bone Marrow Transplant Centre at LUTH
item.description: Nigeria don record major breakthrough for healthcare as West Africa’s first comprehensive sickle cell cure centre don open for Lagos University Teaching Hospital (LUTH) through partnership between Sic...

item.title: Iran Appeals to UN Following US and Israeli Military Strikes
item.description: On February 28, 2026, as military strikes reportedly targeted Tehran, the Iranian Foreign Ministry issued a statement condemning what it described as 'criminal aggression' by the United States and Isr...

item.title: Los Angeles Designates Iconic 'Brady Bunch' House as Historic-Cultural Landmark
item.description: The Los Angeles City Council has officially designated the Studio City home featured in the classic television show 'The Brady Bunch' as a historic-cultural monument, ensuring its preservation and cul...

item.title: 63 Practical Ideas to Embrace Spring for Mind, Body and Home
item.description: Spring has officially arrived, bringing with it warmer days, blossoming flowers, and the chance to refresh our routines. This guide offers 63 practical and enjoyable ways to welcome the new season, co...

item.title: Moi University Improves Student Portal but MUSOMI Platform Still Faces Glitches
item.description: Moi University has rolled out a major revamp of its official website and student portal, with upgrades aimed at enhancing speed, responsiveness, and access to academic records. Students now enjoy fast...

item.title: Western Cape welcomes re-enlistment of 62 experienced detectives to boost SAPS investigative capacity
item.description: Western Cape Premier Alan Winde and MEC for Police Oversight and Community Safety Anroux Marais have welcomed the re-enlistment of 62 seasoned detectives into the South African Police Service (SAPS). ...

item.title: Episode 792 of Batang Quiapo Highlights Tanggol’s Courage and Dedication to the Community
item.description: In Episode 792 of Batang Quiapo, viewers witness Tanggol Montenegro’s unwavering commitment to protecting and serving his community. The episode showcases his moral strength and courage as he confront...

item.title: IISc and Pratiksha Trust begin project to develop AI-powered brain co-processors for stroke rehabilitation
item.description: The Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bengaluru, in collaboration with the Pratiksha Trust, has initiated a pioneering moonshot project aimed at developing brain co-processors that integrate neuromo...

item.title: Tinubu Set to Swear in Tunji Disu as Nigeria’s Inspector-General of Police
item.description: President Bola Tinubu don schedule to swear in Olatunji Rilwan Disu as the new Inspector-General of Police (IGP) after the Nigeria Police Council confirm am on Monday. The confirmation meeting happen ...
